title: See what I can help with – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield
h1: See what I can help with
headings: h2: What I can help with
text_excerpt: See what I can help with – Laurence Turner - working hard for Northfield LAURENCE TURNER Home Latest news and views Contact and support Check if you are a constituent How to get in touch See what I can help with Office standards About me Get updates Donate See what I can help with If you are a constituent in Birmingham Northfield and you are experiencing difficulties then please contact me if you think I can assist. I am here to help and represent you, and I will assist if I can. Please note that Parliament operates under a strict code which means that I can only take up issues on behalf of people who live in the constituency. You can click here to check if I am your MP. What I can help with MPs can help to make representations to public bodies on your behalf when you aren’t getting the right treatment, such as: DWP HMRC The Home Office Birmingham City Council Please include any reference numbers that you hold, and copies of correspondence from those bodies if you have them. Councillors are also elected to hold Birmingham City Council to account and they should be contacted in the first instance on council matters. You can find and contact your councillors here . I might also be able to raise policy issues in Parliament. You can read more here . Surgeries I hold regular surgeries across the constituency. Please contact me and include your postal address and a summary of the issue to request an appointment. A member of the team will be in touch. Many problems can be resolved faster over the phone or by email and my staff may make suggestions on the best way of taking a matter forward. Sources of local support My team can suggest local sources of support, but you may wish to contact those organisations directly: Birmingham City Council : Cost of living support https://www.birmingham.gov.uk/livingsupport Northfield Community Partnership offers high-quality advice and assistance with a wide range of issues, including social housing and benefits matters (not including PIP), and food parcels. 0121 411 2157 - https://www.northfieldcommunity.org/ Birmingham Citizens Advice Bureau can assist with a number of matters, including PIP applications: 0808 278 7990 - https://www.bcabs.org.uk/ Birmingham SENDIASS free advice on special educational needs and disabilities (SEND), arms-length from Birmingham City Council: 0121 303 5004 - https://www.birminghamsendiass.co.uk/ St Bart’s Pantry food pantry in Allen’s Cross: 07568 528 826 - https://www.yourlocalpantry.co.uk/pantry-listings/birmingham-st-bart-s-pantry/ B30 & South Birmingham Foodbank in Kings Norton: 07582 143 972 - https://b30.foodbank.org.uk/ FoodCycle Weoley Castle offers a free weekly hot meal at St Gabriels Church: https://foodcycle.org.uk/location/foodcycle-birmingham-weoley-castle/ NewStarts in Frankley can offer free furniture, household supplies, and food support to those who need it: 0121 679 2072 - https://newstarts.org.uk/ Visiting Parliament I can help with arranging visits to Parliament school visits and getting tickets for Prime Minister’s Questions - although please note that the dates for school visits and PMQs book up months in advance. Promoted by and on behalf of Laurence Turner of Terry Duffy House, 1 Thomas Street, B70 6NT Privacy policy Acceptable use policy Cookie Preferences This website was built at no cost to taxpayers with Quarto
